colourway: Red body, white collar and sleeves
history: The shirt worn during United's historic Treble-winning season — Premier League, FA Cup and Champions League. The comeback win over Bayern Munich in Barcelona on 26 May 1999 made this one of the most iconic kits in football history.

authenticityGuide.description: Should be a clean woven badge on the left chest. Fakes often have a flat printed logo with uneven diamond spacing.
The Sharp lettering uses a specific sans-serif weight. Counterfeit versions frequently have thicker or thinner lettering.
The white ribbed collar should have tight, even stitching with no loose threads or puckering at the join.
Genuine shirts have a woven Umbro label with size, care instructions in English, and a Premier League hologram tag.

details.Collar style: White ribbed V-neck with red trim
details.Sleeve detail: White sleeve panels with Umbro tape
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label + PL hologram

colourway: White with navy and gold pinstripe
history: The away shirt of the Treble season, worn in several key Champions League group stage matches. The white with navy pinstripe design was considered one of Umbro's finest away kits of the era.

authenticityGuide.description: The navy pinstripes should be printed precisely and evenly spaced. On fakes they are often blurred or inconsistently spaced.
The Umbro logo appears in gold on the away kit. Cheap reproductions often use yellow rather than true gold.
Original fabric has a light mesh texture. Counterfeits are often made of a smoother, shinier polyester.
Sharp sponsor appears in navy on the white shirt. The lettering should be crisp with no colour bleed.

details.Collar style: White ribbed crew neck with navy trim
details.Sleeve detail: White sleeves with gold Umbro tape
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label + PL hologram

colourway: Red and white with gold detail
history: The Invincibles shirt. Worn by Arsène Wenger's Arsenal side through the entire 2003/04 Premier League season unbeaten — 26 wins and 12 draws across 38 games. Widely considered the most famous Arsenal kit ever produced.

authenticityGuide.description: The club crest features a gold cannon on a red background. On genuine shirts the embroidery is raised and detailed. Fakes often use a flat iron-on transfer.
The O2 logo is printed in white on the red body. The circle and text should be perfectly aligned and proportioned.
Genuine Nike Dri-FIT fabric has a fine hexagonal mesh pattern visible under light. Budget fakes use a plain weave.
Should have a Nike jock tag at the hem and a woven neck label. The Premier League patch appears on the right sleeve.

details.Collar style: Crew neck with red ribbed trim and gold piping
details.Badge style: Embroidered gold cannon crest, left chest
details.Sleeve detail: White sleeve panels, PL patch on right sleeve
details.Tagging: Nike woven neck label, Nike jock tag at hem

colourway: All red with white Adidas stripes
history: Liverpool's title-winning shirt from the last time they won the First Division championship before a 30-year wait. The classic Adidas three-stripe design on a plain red base is one of the purest Liverpool kits ever made.

authenticityGuide.description: The white stripes on the sleeves should be stitched fabric, not printed. Genuine vintage Adidas use sewn-on stripe panels.
This era used the classic Adidas trefoil, not the Equipment or Performance logo. Ensure it is correctly proportioned and positioned.
The sponsor lettering should be an embroidered or heat-pressed block font. Reproductions often use incorrect font weights.
Late 80s Adidas used a cotton-poly blend with a distinctly different weight and drape to modern polyester replicas.

details.Sleeve detail: White Adidas three stripes on sleeves
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label, Made in England

colourway: Black and white stripes
history: The shirt of Newcastle's famous title challenge under Kevin Keegan, when they led the Premier League by 12 points before famously being pipped by Manchester United. Associated with the iconic 4-3 match against Liverpool and Keegan's emotional 'I would love it' speech.

authenticityGuide.description: Genuine Adidas Newcastle shirts have precisely equal black and white stripe widths. Many fakes have slightly uneven stripes.
The Newcastle Brown Ale logo should be a clean woven badge. The eagle and lettering are often poorly reproduced on counterfeits.
Features a black and white striped collar with a small V. The stripe alignment at the collar join is a key authenticity indicator.
This era used the Adidas Equipment logo (three bars). Fakes sometimes incorrectly use the trefoil or Performance logos.

details.Sleeve detail: Adidas Equipment three-bar tape on sleeves
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label + PL hologram tag

colourway: Royal blue with white trim
history: José Mourinho's first season at Chelsea, the year they won the Premier League title with a then-record 95 points. The clean royal blue Umbro design became associated with the arrival of the Special One and Roman Abramovich's era.

authenticityGuide.description: The Emirates wordmark is printed in white. Check the font — it should use Emirates' own custom typeface, often poorly copied.
Woven Umbro double diamond logo on left chest. On fakes the diamond is often slightly misshapen or the weave is too coarse.
Chelsea's embroidered lion crest should have fine detail in the lion's mane. A blurry or flat crest indicates a fake.
Premier League sleeve patch on right sleeve. Check the lion's position and the lettering — a common area where fakes cut corners.

details.Badge style: Embroidered Chelsea lion crest, left chest
details.Sleeve detail: White sleeve panels, PL patch right sleeve
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label + PL hologram

colourway: Yellow body, blue shorts, white socks
history: The shirt of arguably the greatest team ever assembled. Pelé, Jairzinho, Rivellino and Tostão won the 1970 World Cup in Mexico with a brand of football that has never been surpassed. The yellow shirt became the universal symbol of beautiful football.

authenticityGuide.description: The specific canary yellow is distinct from later Brazil kits. Genuine originals have a slightly warmer, deeper yellow than modern reproductions.
Genuine 1970 shirts were made of heavy cotton. Any polyester construction immediately indicates a reproduction rather than an original.
The embroidered Brazilian Football Confederation badge should show fine stitch detail. Original match shirts had hand-stitched crests.
The green V-neck collar trim should be a clean, bright green. On originals this was a separate ribbed trim panel sewn on.

details.Sleeve detail: Plain yellow, no manufacturer branding
details.Tagging: Simple cotton care label, Made in Brazil

colourway: Yellow with green collar and cuffs
history: Ronaldo's redemption. Four years after his mysterious illness before the 1998 final, Ronaldo scored twice in the 2002 World Cup final against Germany in Yokohama, winning his second World Cup and cementing his place among the all-time greats.

authenticityGuide.description: Blue Nike swoosh on the right chest. Should be a clean embroidered or heat-applied logo — check proportions carefully.
The green and gold shield badge should have crisp embroidery with fine detail. Blurring or colour bleed indicates a fake.
Authentic Nike Dri-FIT has a distinctive fine-knit texture. Many Brazil reproductions use a plain smooth polyester.
The four stars above the badge (one for each World Cup) must be precisely spaced and aligned. Uneven stars are a common giveaway.

details.Collar style: Green V-neck with yellow ribbed trim
details.Sleeve detail: Green cuffs, no sponsor on sleeves
details.Tagging: Nike woven neck label, FIFA World Cup label

colourway: Deep navy blue with gold trim
history: France's World Cup winning shirt on home soil. Zinedine Zidane's two headers in the final against Brazil secured a 3-0 win and France's first World Cup. Worn on the night that a generation of French fans will never forget.

authenticityGuide.description: The French blue (bleu nuit) is a very deep, dark navy. Counterfeits frequently use a brighter or lighter blue.
The French Football Federation badge features a gold cockerel. Fine feather detail in the embroidery distinguishes originals from fakes.
Gold three stripes on the shoulders. These should be a clean, warm gold — not yellow. Many fakes use the wrong shade.
Player issue shirts carry an official FIFA World Cup woven label. The font and FIFA logo on this label is frequently wrong on counterfeits.

details.Collar style: Crew neck with gold ribbed trim
details.Sleeve detail: Gold Adidas three stripes on shoulders
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label, FFF label

colourway: Azzurro blue with white collar
history: Italy's fourth World Cup winning shirt. Marcello Lippi's side beat France on penalties in Berlin after a 1-1 draw, with Fabio Cannavaro lifting the trophy. Zidane's headbutt on Materazzi was the tournament's defining moment.

authenticityGuide.description: Italy's specific sky blue (azzurro) is a lighter, brighter blue than France's navy. The exact shade is frequently wrong on counterfeits.
The Italian Football Federation badge features a four-quarters shield. The fine detail of the shield divisions is a good authenticity check.
The leaping Puma cat should be a clean, consistent shape on the right chest. Distortion in the cat's silhouette indicates a poor reproduction.
The white V-neck collar trim is a distinctive feature. On originals, the collar is a separate ribbed piece with clean join stitching.

details.Collar style: White V-neck with fine blue piping
details.Sleeve detail: Plain, Italian flag patch on right sleeve
details.Tagging: Woven Puma neck label, World Cup authenticity tag

colourway: White with blue trim
history: The white away shirt worn during Italy's 2006 World Cup campaign. Notably worn in the semi-final against Germany in Dortmund, where Italy won 2-0 in extra time with two goals in the final two minutes from Grosso and Del Piero.

authenticityGuide.description: Thin blue piping runs around the collar and cuffs. On genuine shirts this piping is clean and unbroken. Fakes often have thicker or uneven piping.
The badge appears on a white background on the away shirt. Check that the azzurro and red shield sections are the correct shades.
Same ActvChill fabric as the home shirt. The fine knit should be visible and consistent across the shirt with no loose threads.
The woven size label should specify S/M/L/XL clearly. Poor counterfeit labels often have blurry or incorrectly formatted text.

details.Sleeve detail: Plain white, Italian flag patch right sleeve
details.Tagging: Woven Puma neck label

colourway: Vivid orange with black Adidas stripes
history: The Netherlands' European Championship winning shirt, with Marco van Basten scoring one of the greatest goals in football history — a volley from an almost impossible angle — in the final against the Soviet Union. The generation of Gullit, van Basten and Rijkaard at their peak.

authenticityGuide.description: The shade of orange is very specific — a warm, vivid tangerine. Fakes frequently use a less saturated or more yellow-toned orange.
Black three stripes on the shoulders, sewn as separate fabric strips. Any printed or transfer stripes indicate a reproduction.
The Royal Dutch Football Association badge features a lion on an orange background. Original embroidery has clear claw and mane detail.
This era used the classic Adidas trefoil logo, positioned on the right chest. Check proportions and number of trefoil leaves.

details.Badge style: Embroidered KNVB lion crest, left chest
details.Sleeve detail: Black Adidas three stripes on shoulders
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label, Made in West Germany

colourway: White with broad red centre stripe
history: The shirt worn by Louis van Gaal's extraordinary Ajax side that won the Champions League, defeating AC Milan 1-0 in the final in Vienna. The squad included a 17-year-old Patrick Kluivert, plus Clarence Seedorf, Edgar Davids and the De Boer twins.

authenticityGuide.description: The single broad red stripe runs centrally from collar to hem. Precise width is important — too wide or too narrow indicates a poor copy.
The embroidered Ajax profile head crest should have clean, detailed linework. The portrait's features are a common area of inaccuracy on fakes.
The sponsor text is an embroidered wordmark in red on white. Letter spacing and font weight are frequently wrong on counterfeits.
Classic Umbro double diamond on left chest. Woven construction — printed or loose-thread diamonds indicate a fake.

details.Badge style: Embroidered Ajax head profile, left chest
details.Sleeve detail: White sleeves with fine red side stripe
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label + UEFA label

colourway: Red and black vertical stripes
history: The shirt worn by Arrigo Sacchi's legendary Milan side that won the European Cup, beating Steaua Bucharest 4-0 in the final. The squad featured van Basten, Gullit and Rijkaard — the Dutch trio who had just won Euro 88 with the Netherlands.

authenticityGuide.description: Red and black vertical stripes should be exactly equal in width and perfectly aligned at the shoulders and hem seams.
White Adidas stripes on the shoulders. Genuine vintage Adidas used sewn fabric strips rather than printed or transfer applications.
The Mediolanum lettering uses a specific serif font. This sponsor is often replaced with modern counterfeits that use the wrong typeface.
Left chest trefoil logo. On late 80s Adidas the trefoil leaves have a distinct shape — slightly longer and more pointed than modern copies.

details.Collar style: V-neck with red and black trim
details.Sleeve detail: White Adidas three stripes on shoulders
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label, Made in West Germany

colourway: Blue and red vertical stripes
history: Pep Guardiola's first season as Barcelona manager, the year they won the historic Treble — La Liga, Copa del Rey and Champions League, beating Manchester United in the final in Rome. The debut season of the Messi-Xavi-Iniesta era at its peak.

authenticityGuide.description: The blaugrana stripes must align precisely at all seams. Nike's genuine construction ensures perfect alignment — fakes frequently have misaligned stripes.
Barcelona famously carried no commercial sponsor at this time, displaying Unicef instead. Any commercial sponsor on this shirt indicates a wrong era or fake.
The Barcelona crest features St George's cross, the Catalan flag, and a ball. The fine embroidery detail, especially in the flag quarters, distinguishes originals.
La Liga patch on the sleeve. Check that the LFP lettering and lion are correct for the 2008/09 season format.

details.Sleeve detail: Plain, La Liga patch on right sleeve
details.Tagging: Nike woven neck label, Nike jock tag at hem

colourway: Blue and white halves
history: The shirt worn when Blackburn Rovers won their only Premier League title, pipping Manchester United on the final day. Kenny Dalglish's side, bankrolled by Jack Walker, ended an 81-year wait for the top flight title. Alan Shearer scored 34 goals.

authenticityGuide.description: The blue and white half design should have a clean, straight vertical dividing line. Misaligned halves are an instant fake indicator.
Asics used their distinctive parallel bars logo in this era. This is a less commonly faked brand so genuine originals are more readily found.
The McEwan's Lager sponsor uses a specific serif script. This is an unusual sponsor rarely reproduced accurately on counterfeits.
Should carry the Premier League hologram tag on the back collar, introduced for the 1994/95 season.

details.Collar style: White polo collar with blue trim
details.Sleeve detail: Asics logo tape on sleeves
details.Tagging: Woven Asics neck label + PL hologram

colourway: White with navy and red trim
history: Italia 90 — England's most beloved tournament in a generation. Paul Gascoigne's tears after the semi-final booking against West Germany, Lineker's late equaliser, and then heartbreak on penalties. The shirt carries the emotion of an entire nation.

authenticityGuide.description: The embroidered Three Lions badge should show clear individual lions with visible claw and mane detail. A blurry or flat badge indicates a fake.
Umbro's signature chest stripe — a thin horizontal tape across the chest in navy and red. Often incorrectly coloured or proportioned on fakes.
Woven Umbro double diamond neck label. The label should specify FA approved and include care washing instructions.
1990 Umbro shirts used a heavier cotton blend. Purely synthetic replicas don't replicate the correct weight or drape of the original.

details.Collar style: White polo collar with navy trim
details.Sleeve detail: White sleeves, Umbro diamond badge on sleeve
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label, FA approval label

colourway: White with black and gold trim
history: West Germany's World Cup winning shirt in Italia 90, their third and final World Cup as West Germany before reunification. Lothar Matthäus lifted the trophy after a 1-0 win over Argentina, with Andreas Brehme scoring the only goal from the penalty spot.

authenticityGuide.description: The German Football Association eagle badge should show fine feather detail. The eagle's wingspan and claw position are commonly wrong on fakes.
Black three stripes on the shoulders as separate sewn fabric strips. Any printed application indicates a reproduction.
Gold piping runs around the collar and cuffs. Should be a warm, true gold — fakes frequently use a yellower or duller metallic.
The trefoil appears on the right chest at a specific size. Wrong placement or scale is a common indicator of a poor reproduction.

details.Collar style: White polo collar with black and gold trim
details.Sleeve detail: Black Adidas three stripes on shoulders
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label, Made in West Germany

colourway: Green and white hoops
history: Martin O'Neill's Celtic side won the Scottish Premier League and reached the UEFA Cup final in Seville, losing 3-2 to Porto in extra time. The Seville final attracted over 80,000 Celtic fans to Spain — one of the largest football supporter travel events in history.

authenticityGuide.description: Celtic's hoops should be equal width — approximately 7 green and 7 white. Incorrect hoop count or unequal widths indicate a fake.
The Celtic crest features a four-leaf clover. The detailed shamrock stitching in the embroidery is a good authenticity marker.
The NTL logo uses a specific green on green design. Often reproduced incorrectly — the shade of green used for the lettering should closely match the hoops.
Woven Umbro diamond on left chest. Should sit at a specific height on the chest — too high or low indicates a counterfeit.

details.Collar style: White V-neck with green ribbed trim
details.Badge style: Embroidered four-leaf clover crest, left chest
details.Sleeve detail: White sleeves, Umbro logo on sleeve
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label + SPL label

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes
history: Marcello Lippi's first season as Juventus manager, finishing second in Serie A. The foundation of the side that would win back-to-back Champions Leagues in 1995 and 1996. Del Piero was emerging, and Vialli and Ravanelli were at their peak.

authenticityGuide.description: Black and white equal-width stripes must align perfectly at all seams. Poor alignment at the shoulders is the most common fake indicator on Juventus shirts.
Kappa's two seated figures logo should be a clean, precise design. The silhouettes' proportions are frequently wrong on counterfeit products.
The Danone waves logo uses a specific shade of blue. On genuine shirts the logo is a heat-applied or woven badge with clean edges.
Carries a Serie A official licensing tag. The black stripes on this tag should match the shirt's black exactly.

details.Badge style: Embroidered Juventus oval badge, left chest
details.Sleeve detail: Kappa taping on sleeves
details.Tagging: Woven Kappa neck label + Serie A tag

colourway: All white with yellow and blue trim
history: Leeds United's first season as reigning First Division champions in the new Premier League era. Howard Wilkinson's side had won the title in 1991/92 in the final season before the Premier League, making them the last ever First Division champions.

authenticityGuide.description: Admiral's distinctive yellow and blue trim on collar and cuffs. The specific sequence of colours and width of each trim band should be checked carefully.
Admiral's distinctive chevron logo. On genuine shirts this is an embroidered or woven badge — any printed application indicates a reproduction.
Top Man lettering in blue on the white shirt. Fakes of this shirt are uncommon but the sponsor font is a good check point.
The embroidered Leeds badge features a white rose of Yorkshire. The petals should have clear individual definition in the embroidery.

details.Collar style: White polo collar with yellow and blue trim
details.Badge style: Embroidered Leeds rose badge, left chest
details.Sleeve detail: Yellow and blue Admiral chevron trim
details.Tagging: Woven Admiral neck label

colourway: Light blue and white vertical stripes
history: The shirt Maradona wore when he single-handedly won the World Cup in Mexico. The quarter-final against England included both the Hand of God and the Goal of the Century in the same match. The tournament's defining moments all came in this sky-blue-and-white shirt.

authenticityGuide.description: Argentina's classic light blue (celeste) is a very specific, pale sky blue. Too dark or too grey indicates an inaccurate reproduction.
Genuine 1986 match shirts were heavy cotton. Any synthetic fabric immediately identifies a modern reproduction rather than an original.
The Argentine Football Association badge features a sun and a football. Original match shirt crests were hand-embroidered with fine stitch work.
The French cockerel logo of Le Coq Sportif should be correctly proportioned. This manufacturer is less commonly faked but reproductions do circulate.

details.Collar style: Light blue V-neck with white trim
details.Sleeve detail: Plain, no manufacturer shoulder branding
details.Tagging: Simple cotton care label

colourway: Sky blue with white trim
history: The shirt from the most dramatic title win in Premier League history. Sergio Agüero's stoppage-time winner against QPR on the final day — City needing two goals in injury time to pip Manchester United on goal difference. 'AGUEROOOO' became the most famous piece of football commentary in Premier League history.

authenticityGuide.description: The Etihad Airways logo features a specific shade of dark teal. Check the proportions and font of the wordmark — it is often poorly reproduced.
Manchester City's eagle crest should have fine feather and shield detail. The red ship in the crest quarter is a common area of inaccuracy.
City's exact sky blue is a bright, clean light blue. Too dark or too green indicates a wrong shade fabric.
The PL lion patch on the right sleeve. Check the lion's posture and the EPL lettering format for the 2011/12 season.

details.Collar style: White crew neck with sky blue trim
details.Sleeve detail: White sleeve panels, PL patch right sleeve
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label + PL hologram

colourway: Deep red with green trim
history: Portugal's third-place finish at the 2006 World Cup in Germany, their best tournament result since 1966. Cristiano Ronaldo won the Golden Boot with 8 goals, though his theatrics against England in the quarter-final made him a villain to some England fans.

authenticityGuide.description: Portugal's red is a deep, slightly darker red than England's. The specific shade is distinctive — too bright or too orange indicates a wrong fabric.
The Portuguese Federation shield features the quinas — five blue shields arranged in a cross pattern. The tiny dots within each shield are a detail fakes often miss.
Green appears on the collar and cuffs. Portugal's national colours require a specific forest green, not lime or apple green.
Carries an official FIFA World Cup 2006 Nike label. The Germany 2006 logo format on this label is frequently wrong on fake World Cup shirts.

details.Collar style: Crew neck with green ribbed trim
details.Sleeve detail: Plain, no sleeve sponsor
details.Tagging: Nike woven neck label, FIFA World Cup 2006 tag

colourway: All white with gold detail
history: The shirt from Real Madrid's eighth European Cup / Champions League title, defeating Valencia 3-0 in the final in Paris. Steve McManaman scored a stunning volley, and the shirt marked the peak of the Florentino Pérez-era Galácticos beginning to form.

authenticityGuide.description: Real Madrid's white must be a pure, cool white — not cream or off-white. Any warmth in the white shade indicates a lower quality reproduction.
The Teka kitchen appliances logo uses a specific sans-serif font and blue colour. Rarely faked accurately as it's an unusual sponsor for modern counterfeiters.
The Madrid crest features a detailed crown and the initials MCF. Fine embroidery detail in the crown's jewels is a strong authenticity indicator.
This era used the Adidas Performance three-stripe logo, not the trefoil or Equipment mark. Check that the correct era logo is present.

details.Badge style: Embroidered Real Madrid crest, left chest
details.Sleeve detail: Adidas three-stripe tape on sleeves
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label + LFP label

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes, black V-neck collar
history: Kevin Keegan's first full season in charge after saving the club from relegation. Newcastle finished 11th in the old Second Division, laying foundations for the dramatic promotion push the following year.

authenticityGuide.description: Woven double diamond on the left chest. Early 90s Umbro diamonds have specific proportions that differ from later versions.
Black and white stripes should be perfectly equal in width and align cleanly at all seams. Uneven stripes indicate a fake.
The Newcastle Brown Ale star-and-circle logo should be cleanly applied with crisp edges. Blurred or smudged logos indicate a counterfeit.
Early 90s Umbro used a heavier cotton-polyester blend. Purely synthetic reproductions feel noticeably lighter.

details.Collar style: Black V-neck with white ribbed trim
details.Sleeve detail: Umbro diamond on sleeve
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label

colourway: Yellow with black trim and black Umbro stripes
history: The away strip for Newcastle's Second Division survival season. Yellow has long been Newcastle's traditional away colour and this early Umbro design established that identity.

authenticityGuide.description: A warm, mid-tone canary yellow. Too bright or too orange indicates an incorrect reproduction.
Woven double diamond on left chest. Same proportions and construction as the home shirt.
Black collar and cuff trim should be a separate ribbed piece with clean join stitching, not a printed detail.
The Brown Ale badge should have clear contrast against the yellow base. Poor reproductions have a washed-out badge.

details.Sleeve detail: Black Umbro sleeve detail
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes, white polo collar
history: The shirt worn as Newcastle won the newly-renamed First Division title under Kevin Keegan, earning promotion to the inaugural Premier League season. Andy Cole finished with 12 goals as the club returned to top-flight football.

authenticityGuide.description: Woven double diamond on left chest. Slightly updated from 1991/92 with a marginally cleaner weave finish.
This season features a white collar — an important distinction from the previous year's black collar. Black-collared versions are incorrect.
Equal-width stripes with clean alignment at all seams. Tight, uniform stitching throughout.
Genuine shirts have a woven label with manufacturer, size and fabric composition. Missing or incorrect info indicates a fake.

details.Sleeve detail: Umbro tape detail on sleeves
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label

colourway: Yellow with black polo collar and black Umbro trim
history: The away shirt for the First Division title-winning campaign. Yellow remained the colour of choice as Keegan's side clinched promotion back to the top flight.

authenticityGuide.description: Canary yellow should be even across the entire shirt. Fading or uneven dye distribution is common on aged reproductions.
Woven diamond badge on left chest, consistent with the home shirt's badge construction.
Black polo collar with tight ribbed construction. This season's collar is slightly cleaner in form than 1991/92.
The Brown Ale badge sits centrally on the chest. Correct vertical placement is important — too high or low indicates a counterfeit.

details.Sleeve detail: Black Umbro sleeve detail
details.Tagging: Woven Umbro neck label

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black Adidas shoulder stripes
history: Newcastle's first Premier League season, and Andy Cole's breakout campaign — he scored 34 league goals, a record at the time. The move to Adidas began a long iconic partnership and the club finished third in the table.

authenticityGuide.description: This era used the Adidas Equipment three-bar logo, not the trefoil or Performance mark. Incorrect logo is an immediate fake indicator.
Black Adidas three stripes on the shoulder seams, sewn as separate fabric strips. Printed or heat-applied stripes indicate a fake.
The vertical black and white stripes must align precisely at every seam. Any offset at shoulders or sides is an immediate fake indicator.
Premier League hologram authentication tag introduced for the first PL season. Its presence and correct format are essential.

details.Collar style: Black and white striped V-neck collar
details.Sleeve detail: Black Adidas three-stripe tape on shoulders
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label + PL hologram

colourway: Yellow with black Adidas shoulder stripes and black trim
history: The away shirt for Newcastle's debut Premier League season. Yellow remained the traditional away colour as the club established themselves in the top flight under Keegan with a fine third-place finish.

authenticityGuide.description: The Equipment three-bar logo on the right chest must be correct for this era. Performance or trefoil logos indicate a wrong-era shirt.
Sewn black three-stripe panels on the shoulders. Must be fabric strips, not printed, to be genuine Adidas construction.
Adidas used a clean mid-tone yellow slightly warmer in tone than Umbro's version.
Premier League hologram authentication tag on the back collar, introduced for this inaugural PL season.

details.Sleeve detail: Black Adidas three-stripe tape on shoulders
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label + PL hologram

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes, white V-neck collar and cuffs
history: A season disrupted by the controversial sale of Andy Cole to Manchester United in January 1995. Despite the upheaval Newcastle finished sixth, and Les Ferdinand was already signed for the summer — signalling the ambition that would almost deliver the title in 1995/96.

authenticityGuide.description: Equipment three-bar logo on the right chest. Same placement and proportions as 1993/94.
This season introduced a white collar and cuffs, distinguishing it from 1993/94. Black-collared versions claiming to be 1994/95 are wrong.
Equal-width black and white stripes with tight seam stitching. Even minor width differences indicate a poor reproduction.
Second season of the PL hologram tag. The hologram format changed slightly from 1993/94 — check the specific version.

details.Collar style: White ribbed V-neck with black trim
details.Sleeve detail: Black Adidas three-stripe tape on shoulders
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label + PL hologram

colourway: Yellow body with navy blue Adidas stripes and navy trim
history: The away shirt for a season of frustration and ambition in equal measure. Despite Cole's sale, the club's horizons were expanding rapidly under Keegan.

authenticityGuide.description: The contrast between yellow body and navy trim should be strong. Faded or wrong-shade navy indicates a lower quality reproduction.
Navy Equipment logo on the right chest. On the yellow away shirt the logo should stand out clearly — poor application causes bleeding.
Navy three-stripe panels sewn on the yellow shoulders. Fabric application, not print — check by feel.
Navy ribbed collar should be a separate sewn piece. The stitch line where it meets the body is a good authenticity check.

details.Sleeve detail: Navy Adidas three-stripe tape on shoulders
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label + PL hologram

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black and white striped polo collar
history: One of the most iconic shirts in Premier League history, worn during the famous title challenge. Newcastle led the table by 12 points at Christmas before finishing runners-up to Manchester United. Kevin Keegan's 'I would love it if we beat them' outburst is one of football's defining moments. Les Ferdinand, David Ginola and Peter Beardsley were at their electrifying peak.

authenticityGuide.description: Equipment three-bar logo, right chest. One of the most faked Newcastle shirts — ensure the Equipment version is used, not trefoil or Performance.
Distinctive black and white striped polo collar mirroring the shirt itself. Solid black or white collars claiming to be 1995/96 are wrong.
The vertical stripes should flow continuously into the collar stripes without interruption. This alignment is notoriously difficult to replicate accurately.
The Brown Ale badge sits centrally between the crest and bottom hem. Precise vertical positioning is a key authenticity check.

details.Collar style: Black and white striped polo collar
details.Sleeve detail: Black Adidas three-stripe tape on shoulders
details.Tagging: Woven Adidas neck label + PL hologram

colourway: Yellow with black Adidas stripes and black collar
history: The away shirt for the iconic title challenge season. Worn in memorable away fixtures including the extraordinary 4-3 defeat at Anfield — often cited as the greatest Premier League game ever played.

authenticityGuide.description: Clean yellow body with black details is distinctive. Check that the yellow exactly matches reference images for this specific season.
Black Equipment logo on the yellow body. On genuine shirts the logo is clean and slightly raised — flat or bleeding logos indicate a fake.
Black sewn three-stripe panels on the shoulders. The stripe fabric weight should feel the same as the body.
Premier League hologram tag on the back collar. The 1995/96 format is specific — check against reference images.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black collar and gold Adidas detailing
history: Alan Shearer's first season at Newcastle after his world record £15m transfer from Blackburn Rovers. Kevin Keegan resigned in January 1997 and was replaced by Kenny Dalglish. Despite the upheaval, the season ended with a run to the FA Cup final — a campaign defined by Shearer's immediate impact.

authenticityGuide.description: Gold appears in the Adidas branding and collar trim this season — distinguishing 1996/97 from the all-black detailing of 1995/96. Missing gold indicates a wrong-era shirt.
Equipment three-bar logo with gold accent. The subtle gold on the Equipment mark is a key dating detail.
Same high-quality Adidas stripe construction as previous seasons. The third year of this template — check all seams for tight, consistent stitching.
Same woven Brown Ale badge as previous seasons. The star-and-ring design should be clearly defined with tight stitching.

details.Collar style: Black V-neck with gold Adidas trim
details.Sleeve detail: Black Adidas three-stripe tape with gold accent

colourway: Navy blue with gold Adidas stripes and gold trim
history: A move away from the traditional yellow — this navy and gold away kit is one of the most popular in the club's history. Worn in the FA Cup run that took Newcastle all the way to Wembley.

authenticityGuide.description: Deep navy with warm gold. The specific shade of gold is important; too yellow or too bronze indicates an inaccurate reproduction.
Gold Equipment three-bar logo on the navy body. The gold should match the stripe colour precisely — a common inconsistency on fakes.
Gold sewn three-stripe panels on navy shoulders. The gold fabric should match the collar and trim exactly.
PL hologram on the back collar. Check the 1996/97 format — it differs from 1995/96.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with white V-neck collar
history: Kenny Dalglish's Newcastle reached the FA Cup final but lost 2-0 to Arsenal's Double-winning side. Shearer was in fine form but the team lacked the creative brilliance of the Keegan era. This season saw the transition from Adidas Equipment to Adidas Performance branding.

authenticityGuide.description: Newcastle transitioned to the Adidas Performance (three-bar) logo this season, replacing the Equipment mark. An Equipment logo on this shirt would be wrong.
A white V-neck collar replaced the gold-trimmed black of 1996/97. Collar colour is a key dating tool across this entire Adidas era.
Consistent stripe quality from previous Adidas seasons but on an updated template. Check stitching pattern at seams.
The Premier League sleeve patch updated for 1997/98. The format differs from the 1996/97 version — check against reference images.

colourway: Yellow with black Adidas stripes and black trim
history: Yellow returned as the away colour for the FA Cup final season. The away kit was worn in several notable league fixtures as Newcastle maintained a mid-table Premier League position while their cup run continued.

authenticityGuide.description: Same transition to Performance logo as the home shirt. Check for the three-bar Performance mark rather than the Equipment version.
Adidas used a slightly brighter yellow in this era compared to earlier seasons. The shade evolved between the 1994/95 and 1997/98 away kits.
Clean black three-stripe panels on yellow shoulders. The contrast between black stripes and yellow body should be sharp.
Updated PL patch for 1997/98. The patch format is a key dating tool when distinguishing this shirt from the 1995/96 away.

colourway: Navy blue with yellow Adidas stripes and yellow trim
history: A navy and yellow third kit used when home and away caused colour clashes. The combination revisited the popular palette from the 1996/97 away, giving fans a further opportunity to own that much-loved colourway.

authenticityGuide.description: Deep navy tone against warm yellow trim. Washed-out navy or wrong-shade yellow is the most common reproduction error.
Yellow Performance three-bar logo on the navy body. Same logo transition as the home and away shirts this season.
Sewn yellow three-stripe panels on navy shoulders. Should match the collar and trim colour exactly.
Genuine third kits carry the same neck label as home and away. Some reproductions use a different label format.

details.Sleeve detail: Yellow Adidas three-stripe tape on shoulders

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black crew neck collar
history: Ruud Gullit's ill-fated season, overshadowed by his decision to drop Alan Shearer and Duncan Ferguson for the Tyne-Wear derby — a game Newcastle lost 2-1. Gullit was sacked the following day. Bobby Robson was eventually appointed and began transforming the club's fortunes.

authenticityGuide.description: Performance three-bar logo consistent with 1997/98. Same placement and proportions as the previous season.
A crew neck replaced the V-neck of 1997/98 — an important distinguishing feature. V-neck versions claiming to be 1998/99 are incorrect.
Same Adidas stripe quality as previous seasons. Any degradation in stripe evenness or seam alignment should be investigated.
The Brown Ale badge continued this season. Check the specific version matches 1998/99 reference imagery.

colourway: Grey with black Adidas stripes and black trim
history: A distinctive grey away kit for the turbulent Gullit season. Grey was fashionable across the Premier League in the late 90s and this is a relatively rare Newcastle colourway.

authenticityGuide.description: A mid-tone silver-grey, not too light or dark. The specific shade is hard to replicate — many fakes use a slightly wrong tone.
Black three-stripe panels on grey shoulders. The contrast between the two colours should be sharp and even.
Black Performance three-bar logo on the grey body. Same construction as the home shirt.
The crew neck collar should be the same grey as the body fabric, not a slightly different shade — a common production error on reproductions.

details.Sleeve detail: Black Adidas three-stripe tape, PL patch on sleeve

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with white V-neck collar
history: Bobby Robson's first full season in charge, transforming the mood at St James' Park after the Gullit disaster. Shearer returned to form and Newcastle finished 11th. Robson's warmth and football intelligence immediately reconnected the club with its supporters.

authenticityGuide.description: Continued Performance three-bar logo. Placement moved slightly compared to 1998/99 — check against reference images.
White V-neck returned after the 1998/99 crew neck. V-neck distinguishes this from 1998/99; crew neck versions would be incorrect for this season.
Adidas updated their polyester construction for 1999/2000. The fabric has a marginally finer weave than earlier season shirts.
The Premier League patch format updated for 1999/2000. Check against reference images from this specific season.

colourway: Royal blue with white Adidas stripes and white trim
history: A fresh royal blue away for Bobby Robson's first season, departing from the recent yellow and grey traditions. The new manager's energy and optimism was reflected in a clean, modern away design.

authenticityGuide.description: A bright vivid royal blue — not navy and not sky blue. The specific shade is distinctive and often wrong on reproductions.
White three-stripe panels on royal blue shoulders. The contrast should be crisp and even across both strips.
White Performance three-bar logo on the blue body. Check proportions and placement are correct for this era.
Same neck labelling as the home shirt. Some reproductions use a generic Adidas label rather than the season-specific version.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black V-neck collar
history: Robson's second season continued the revival, finishing 11th with greater consistency. Shearer, Dyer and the emerging Laurent Robert were beginning to form a cohesive attacking unit.

authenticityGuide.description: Adidas updated their logo placement and sizing for 2000/01. Check the exact position on the right chest against reference images.
A black V-neck for this season — distinguishing it from the white V-neck of 1999/00. An important detail for collectors.
Adidas began introducing ClimaCool technology around this period. The fabric has a distinct moisture-management texture.
The Newcastle Brown Ale badge continued to evolve subtly. Check the specific version matches 2000/01 reference imagery.

details.Sleeve detail: Adidas three-stripe tape, PL patch on sleeve

colourway: White with black Adidas stripes and black trim
history: A clean all-white away kit for Robson's second season. White away kits were occasionally used by Newcastle and this design offered sharp contrast to the traditional home stripes.

authenticityGuide.description: Newcastle's white away must be a clean, cool white. Cream or off-white tones indicate a poor quality reproduction.
Black three-stripe panels on white shoulders. Bleeding black dye onto white fabric is a fake indicator.
Black Performance logo on the white body. On white shirts the logo precision is very visible — check for sharp edges.
Updated PL patch format for 2000/01. The patch should sit correctly on the right sleeve.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with white crew neck collar
history: Bobby Robson's finest season at the club. Newcastle finished 4th in the Premier League, qualifying for the Champions League — their first appearance in the competition. Craig Bellamy, Kieron Dyer and Laurent Robert were electric going forward. A season that revived memories of the Keegan glory years.

authenticityGuide.description: Performance three-bar logo consistent with 2000/01. Check the right chest for correct placement.
White crew neck for 2001/02. V-neck versions claiming to be this season are incorrect — collar style is a key dating tool.
The same high-quality Adidas stripe construction throughout. Any deviation from equal stripe widths should be investigated.
Player issue shirts from European games carry the UEFA Champions League badge on the sleeve — a highly sought detail for collectors.

details.Sleeve detail: Adidas three-stripe tape, PL patch on sleeve

colourway: Navy blue with gold Adidas stripes and gold trim
history: The away shirt for Newcastle's Champions League qualifying season. The navy and gold palette recalled the popular 1996/97 away kit and was worn in away league fixtures as the club secured their top-four finish.

authenticityGuide.description: Deep navy with warm gold — revisiting the 1996/97 away palette. Each shade must match. Too bright a gold or too light a navy indicates a fake.
Gold three-stripe panels on navy shoulders. Fabric quality should match the home shirt. Check gold consistency against collar and trim.
Gold Performance three-bar logo on the navy body. Application should be clean without any gold bleed onto the navy fabric.
The PL patch on a dark navy background. Check that the patch colours are correct and it sits flat without puckering.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black V-neck collar
history: Newcastle's first Champions League season, reaching the second group stage. Shearer and Bellamy were a dangerous partnership, and the 4-3 victory against Bayer Leverkusen at St James' Park was a European highlight. A season that captured the imagination of the whole city.

authenticityGuide.description: Match-worn and player issue shirts from the CL campaign carry the UEFA badge on the sleeve — among the most collectible Newcastle shirt variations.
Black V-neck for 2002/03. Ensure this matches reference images — the collar style changed between seasons in this era.
By 2002/03 Adidas had refined their polyester construction. The fabric has a fine texture and clean finish.
The Brown Ale badge remains central on the chest. This is one of the last seasons with the original-style badge — check against period imagery.

details.Sleeve detail: Adidas three-stripe tape, PL patch on sleeve

colourway: Amber with black Adidas stripes and black trim
history: A distinctive amber away kit for the Champions League season. The warm amber colourway was used for away league fixtures while the home stripes were worn at St James' in European competition.

authenticityGuide.description: A warm, deep amber — not yellow and not orange. Often reproduced incorrectly — usually too orange or too yellow. Check against period match photography.
Black three-stripe panels on amber body creating strong contrast. Precision of stripe edges is important.
Black Performance three-bar logo on the amber body. Should sit centrally on the right chest at the correct height.
Away shirts used in Champions League games may carry a UEFA CL badge — highly sought-after compared to league-issue versions.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with white V-neck collar and gold piping
history: Newcastle's second consecutive Champions League season, again reaching the second group stage. Alan Shearer passed the 200 Premier League goals mark this season — a landmark moment. The club finished 5th under Bobby Robson.

authenticityGuide.description: A distinctive gold trim was introduced on the white collar for 2003/04 — absent from the previous season. Missing gold piping indicates either a wrong season or a fake.
Player issue Champions League shirts carry the iconic UEFA four-star badge on the sleeve. The star detail is a common area where fakes cut corners.
Adidas maintained consistent stripe quality. This shirt uses a slightly updated template from 2002/03 — check seam alignments throughout.
2003/04 was the penultimate season of Newcastle Brown Ale sponsorship. Check the specific badge version against period imagery.

colourway: Green with black Adidas stripes and black trim
history: A striking green away kit for the Champions League season — a bold departure from yellow or navy. Worn in away league fixtures as Newcastle maintained their European campaign.

authenticityGuide.description: A clean, vivid mid-green. Reproductions frequently use the wrong shade — too dark or too lime.
Strong contrast between green body and black stripe panels. Stripe application should be fabric, not printed, on genuine Adidas.
Black Performance three-bar logo on the green body. Check the right chest for correct placement and proportion.
Green shirts used in European away fixtures may carry the Champions League badge — among the more collectible Newcastle away variations.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black crew neck collar
history: Alan Shearer's final season as a professional footballer, ending his nine-year association with Newcastle. Shearer retired having scored 206 Premier League goals and 148 for the club. The club finished 14th — a disappointing return for what was supposed to be a celebratory farewell for their greatest ever player.

authenticityGuide.description: Shearer number 9 shirts from this campaign are among the most collected Newcastle shirts of the era. Check the PL font on the number 9 is correct.
A black crew neck for 2004/05. V-neck versions claiming to be this season are incorrect — collar style is a key dating tool.
Adidas updated their shirt template for 2004/05 with a slightly slimmer cut. The fit is noticeably different from 2003/04 replicas.
2004/05 was the last season with Newcastle Brown Ale as shirt sponsor after over a decade. The badge on this shirt is historically significant for collectors.

history: The away shirt for Shearer's final season. A clean white design worn in away fixtures as the club struggled to recapture Champions League-era form.

authenticityGuide.description: Pure white, no tinting. On the 2004/05 away shirt the white is particularly clean — yellowing or cream tones indicate heavy wear or a reproduction.
Clean black three-stripe panels. The contrast must be sharp with no colour transfer between fabrics.
Same slimmer 2004/05 Adidas template as the home shirt. Check the cut against reference images — the fit changed this season.
Updated PL patch for 2004/05. The format changed from the previous season — check reference images for the correct version.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black V-neck collar
history: The post-Shearer era began with Graeme Souness as manager and a new shirt sponsor in Northern Rock, ending the long Brown Ale association. Souness was sacked in February 2006. Michael Owen — signed from Real Madrid — scored 16 goals before a serious World Cup injury cut short a promising campaign.

authenticityGuide.description: The Northern Rock bank logo replaced Brown Ale. The Northern Rock typeface is a clean sans-serif — incorrect fonts immediately identify a fake.
Any Brown Ale badge on this shirt is wrong — 2005/06 onwards is definitively Northern Rock. Watch for dishonest sellers re-applying old badges.
V-neck collar with updated styling for 2005/06. The collar construction is slightly slimmer than the 2003/04 version.
PL patch format updated for 2005/06. Check the specific version against reference images from this season.

colourway: Red with black Adidas stripes and black trim
history: A bold red away kit — a significant departure from yellow or navy. Red has rarely been used by Newcastle and this stands out as one of the more unusual entries in the club's away shirt history.

authenticityGuide.description: A strong vivid red — not scarlet and not maroon. The exact shade is unusual for Newcastle and often wrong on reproductions.
White Northern Rock lettering on a red background. The lettering should be crisp with no red bleed.
Black sewn three-stripe panels on the red body. The fabric strips should sit flat without puckering on the shoulder seams.
Same woven Adidas neck label as the home shirt. Any deviation in label format from the home shirt indicates a problem.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with white V-neck collar
history: Glenn Roeder's only full season as manager, ending with 13th place. The club was in transition, without Shearer and without a proven goalscorer. Sam Allardyce was appointed at the end of the season to begin a new chapter.

authenticityGuide.description: Second season of Northern Rock sponsorship. Font and logo are consistent with 2005/06 but check for any minor badge updates.
White V-neck returns for 2006/07 after the black collar of 2005/06. Collar colour is a reliable dating detail in this era.
Adidas introduced Formotion technology in their construction around this period. Player issue shirts have a distinctive cut and material.
The PL patch format updated again for 2006/07. Check period photography to confirm the specific format.

details.Sleeve detail: Adidas three-stripe tape, PL patch on sleeve

colourway: Blue with white Adidas stripes and white trim
history: A royal blue away for the 2006/07 transition season. Away from home Newcastle struggled for consistency under a manager who would leave at the season's end.

authenticityGuide.description: A royal to mid-blue — check against period match photography. The shade sits between navy and sky blue.
White Northern Rock lettering on the blue body. The same sans-serif font as the home shirt — any font differences indicate a counterfeit.
White three-stripe panels on blue shoulders. The white should be clean and pure with sharp contrast against the blue.
Consistent Adidas neck labelling with the home shirt. Match the label format exactly.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black crew neck collar
history: Sam Allardyce's only season before Kevin Keegan returned dramatically in January 2008. Keegan's second spell lasted only eight months before he resigned citing boardroom interference. Northern Rock, Newcastle's shirt sponsor, suffered the first bank run in the UK since 1866.

authenticityGuide.description: Northern Rock suffered a famous bank run in September 2007 while on Newcastle's shirt — giving these shirts unusual historical significance beyond football.
Adidas updated their shirt template for 2007/08 with new technical construction. The fit and fabric feel noticeably different from 2006/07.
A black crew neck for 2007/08. Collar style is a consistent dating tool — compare against period imagery.
Updated PL patch for 2007/08. The format changed from 2006/07 — check the specific version.

colourway: Yellow with black Adidas stripes and black trim
history: Yellow returned as the away colour after a two-season absence, welcomed by supporters. The return to the traditional away colour was a bright spot in a season defined by off-field turbulence.

authenticityGuide.description: Yellow returning after red and blue. The Adidas yellow shade for 2007/08 is slightly different from 90s versions — check against period images.
Dark Northern Rock lettering on the yellow body. The specific shade used for the lettering on yellow differs from other shirt colours.
Black sewn panels on yellow shoulders. Check fabric quality and application — consistent with the home shirt's construction.
2007/08 saw the PL introduce a new patch format — distinct from all previous season patches.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with white V-neck collar
history: The relegated shirt. Newcastle were relegated from the Premier League on the final day, finishing 18th after a dismal campaign under Joe Kinnear and interim manager Chris Hughton. One of the lowest points in the club's modern history.

authenticityGuide.description: The last season of Northern Rock as sponsor in the Premier League. The bank was nationalised in February 2008 — making these shirts historically notable.
White V-neck for 2008/09 — returning after the 2007/08 crew neck. Collar identification is important in this era.
Match-worn shirts from the final months of this season carry exceptional collector value given the historical context of the relegation.
The PL sleeve patch here marks the last time it appeared until promotion in 2010. Check it matches the 2008/09 format exactly.

colourway: Green with black Adidas stripes and black trim
history: The away shirt for the relegated season — a green design echoing the 2003/04 away. Worn during increasingly desperate away fixtures that couldn't prevent the drop to the Championship.

authenticityGuide.description: A clean mid-green similar to the 2003/04 away. The specific shade should be verified against period match photography.
White Northern Rock lettering on the green body. Contrast and font precision consistent with the home shirt.
Black sewn three-stripe panels on the green body. Check fabric application and stripe evenness.
The PL patch on an away shirt from a relegation season. Collectors place value on shirts from historically significant seasons.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with black crew neck collar
history: The Championship shirt. Newcastle bounced back at the first attempt, winning the Championship under Chris Hughton. Kevin Nolan and Andy Carroll were instrumental in a campaign that restored pride after the shock of relegation.

authenticityGuide.description: Carries the Football League Championship sleeve patch rather than the Premier League badge — an essential distinction from seasons before and after.
Championship shirts do not carry the PL hologram tag. Its absence is correct for this season — any PL hologram on this shirt indicates a faked Premier League version.
Adidas maintained the same kit quality for the Championship. Fabric and construction are consistent with the 2008/09 PL shirts.
Shirts from after the title was clinched may carry a champions badge. These are the most collected versions of the 2009/10 home shirt.

colourway: Yellow with black Adidas stripes and black trim
history: Yellow for the Championship promotion campaign. The promotion-winning away kit is a popular collector's item — associated with a season that restored pride after the shock of relegation.

authenticityGuide.description: Football League Championship patch rather than Premier League — essential distinguishing detail between this and the 2007/08 yellow away.
2009/10 was the last season of Northern Rock sponsorship — the nationalised bank being wound down. The last shirts to carry that historically significant badge.
Black sewn three-stripe panels on the yellow shoulders. Same construction as previous Adidas yellow away shirts.
Championship shirts lack the PL hologram tag. Its absence on this shirt is correct.

colourway: Black and white vertical stripes with white V-neck collar
history: Newcastle's return to the Premier League after a one-season absence, now under Puma and with Virgin Money as shirt sponsor. Chris Hughton guided the newly-promoted side to a comfortable 12th place. The move to Puma ended a 17-year Adidas partnership.

authenticityGuide.description: The leaping Puma cat replaces Adidas for the first time in nearly two decades. Cat silhouette proportions are specific — check the leap angle and tail position.
The first Puma shirt looks and feels different from the Adidas years. Fabric construction, label position and logo placement all changed.
Virgin Money's red wordmark is printed centrally. The Virgin Group's specific red shade and font should be checked.

history: Yellow remained the away colour for the promotion return season. The first Puma away maintained the traditional Newcastle palette, reassuring supporters despite the manufacturer change.

authenticityGuide.description: The Puma yellow shade differs slightly from Adidas yellow used in previous seasons. Adidas shirts sold as 2010/11 are incorrect.
Black leaping Puma cat on the yellow body. The cat silhouette should be clean and precise against the yellow background.
First season of Puma's fabric construction for Newcastle. The feel and weave differ from Adidas — a useful authenticity marker.
PL patch back on the sleeve after the Championship year. The 2010/11 format should be confirmed against reference images.

history: Newcastle's finest Premier League season since the Champions League era. Alan Pardew's side finished 5th, qualifying for the Europa League. Papiss Cissé arrived in January and scored 13 goals in 14 games. Cheick Tioté's thunderous volley against Arsenal remains one of the goals of the decade.

authenticityGuide.description: The Puma cat logo is consistent with 2010/11 but the second season saw minor template updates — check the specific 2011/12 fit.
One of the most sought-after Newcastle shirts of the Puma era. Shirts with Papiss Cissé 9 from this season are particularly collected.
Puma updated their shirt construction for 2011/12. The fit is slightly different from the previous season.
Player issue shirts from the Europa League qualification campaign may carry a UEFA Europa League badge.

history: The away shirt for the best season of the Puma era. A blue design with yellow accents, worn in away league fixtures as Newcastle secured their 5th place finish.

authenticityGuide.description: Dark blue with yellow trim accents. The yellow used for the Puma branding should match the trim exactly in shade.
Yellow leaping Puma cat on the blue body. The cat silhouette should stand out clearly against the blue background.
Consistent Puma construction with the home shirt. Fabric weight and weave should match when comparing the two shirts.
PL patch on the sleeve. The blue background makes patch placement very visible — check it sits correctly.

The Wonga orange stands out strongly against the stripes. Ensure the logo sits centrally and the orange is consistent in shade.
Puma updated their template for 2012/13. Fit and collar style changed from the previous season.
Early-season shirts from the Europa League campaign carry the UEFA Europa League sleeve badge.

history: Yellow returned as the away colour under the new Wonga sponsorship. The away kit was worn in Europa League fixtures as well as league games.

authenticityGuide.description: The Wonga orange logo on a yellow body — orange on yellow is a challenging combination to reproduce accurately. Check both shades carefully.
Black leaping Puma cat consistent with previous Puma yellow away kits. Check the specific 2012/13 placement against reference images.
Puma's yellow should be consistent across the 2012/13 range. Compare with any yellow trim on the home shirt.
Away shirts used in Europa League fixtures carry the UEFA badge — the more collectible versions of the 2012/13 away kit.

authenticityGuide.description: Second season of Wonga sponsorship. The logo format is consistent with 2012/13 — check for any minor updates to the badge between seasons.
Black crew neck for 2013/14. Compare against the white V-neck of 2012/13 — collar style is a reliable dating tool.
Puma continued to refine their construction. The 2013/14 shirt has a slightly different feel from 2012/13.
Consistent PL patch format. Check the specific version used for 2013/14 against reference imagery.

history: A green away for 2013/14 — revisiting the green colourway from 2003/04 and 2008/09. Used in away league fixtures as the team consolidated a mid-table position.

authenticityGuide.description: A vivid mid-green — consistent with previous green Newcastle aways but in Puma's specific shade, which is slightly more saturated than Adidas versions.
Orange Wonga logo on a green body — orange on green is an unusual combination. Check that colour separation is clean without bleed.
Black Puma cat and black trim on the green body. Consistent construction with the home shirt.
PL sleeve patch in the correct 2013/14 format.
